title: Services for UKHSA staff: UKHSA Knowledge and Library Services
body: "UKHSA staff have access to all services provided by Knowledge and Library Services, including:   Access to resources   Access databases, journals, and eBooks using your UKHSA login. Start at MyAthens (UKHSA) to make sure you are logged in with your UKHSA account. You can use Single Sign-On. You can then access:     Discovery Search: find a specific item (such as an article or book) or browse results on a search topic from UKHSA subscribed resources, NHS core content, and open access resources    BrowZine:  to browse a journal tables of contents   Resources list to select a specific platform/publisher you require     You can search the KLS library catalogue to find books, eBooks and print journals. For more information, visit our dedicated page on accessing journals and articles.   Expert literature searching and information retrieval   A responsive mediated  literature searching service  is available. This includes support to conduct systematic reviews.    Request journal articles   You can request the full text of an article if it is not available through OpenAthens. Visit our access journals and articles page for more information.   Knowledge and Evidence Specialists Contact your Knowledge and Evidence Specialist for identifying and retrieving the latest and most relevant evidence, quality assessment of evidence, and training in information, digital and health literacy.  Training and events  A range of information skills training is available, covering topics such as evidence searching and reference management. For more information, view our training page.   Current awareness alerts  We provide current awareness alerts to help you stay up to date with research in a range of fields.   Knowledge management and mobilisation services      We lead on a range of knowledge management services, including peer assists, after-action reviews and support for communities of practice.   Rapid reviews for UKHSA staff   The Science Evidence Review team produce rapid reviews and provide methodological support to conduct your own evidence review.   Copyright and publishing   We provide guidance on copyright licensing. UKHSA staff can find research support and information about open access publishing, on the intranet.     UKHSA site libraries   Staffed library spaces are located at the Chilton, Colindale and Porton UKHSA sites. All libraries offer study spaces and comfortable seating to enable learning, collaboration and private study. Each library offers specialist book and journal collections.  Borrow print books   Browse our book collection using the KLS library catalogue to find items you would like to borrow.  Visit our page on borrowing books for more information.  Special book collections  UKHSA staff have access to our special book collections:   Wellbeing Collection Learning and Development Collection   Contact us  For further information or queries, contact us.  Get in touch   Feedback This is a new service. Help us improve it by providing feedback.  "
